stage_title: 内存管理（物理/虚拟/分配器）
evidence_id_policy: codex_ev_Qxx_xxx_NN; evidence is local to each question

evidence.quote: RUSTSBI_BASE=0x80000000, KERNBASE=0x80020000, PHYSTOP=0x80600000, VUMMAP=0x70000000, MAXUVA=RUSTSBI_BASE.
evidence.relevance: Defines physical memory bounds and user virtual layout constants.

evidence.quote: struct run has next,npage; struct pm_allocator has spinlock lock, run *freelist, npage; global multiple and single allocators.
evidence.relevance: Physical page allocator state is a run-list protected by two pool locks.

evidence.quote: walk allocates lower-level page tables with allocpage and writes PTE_V; mappages writes PA2PTE(pa)|perm|PTE_V; unmappages optionally freepage and clears *pte.
evidence.relevance: Core page-table walk/map/unmap implementation mutates PTEs.

evidence.quote: kvminit allocates kernel_pagetable and kvmmap maps devices, kernel text/data, trampoline; kvminithart writes satp then sfence.vma.
evidence.relevance: Shows kernel virtual-memory setup and MMU activation.

evidence.quote: handle_excp dispatches store/load/instruction page exceptions to handle_page_fault(kind, r_stval()).
evidence.relevance: Trap layer calls the memory fault handler.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

answer.value: C/Makefile 风格内核（xv6 类）
answer.notes: 内存管理主体是 C 源码中的 xv6 风格物理页、页表和 trap 路径；未见 Rust no_std 或多 crate 内存子系统。

evidence.quote: RUSTSBI_BASE=0x80000000, KERNBASE=0x80020000, PHYSTOP=0x80600000, VUMMAP=0x70000000, MAXUVA=RUSTSBI_BASE.

evidence.quote: struct run has next,npage; struct pm_allocator has spinlock lock, run *freelist, npage; global multiple and single allocators.
evidence.relevance: Physical page allocator state is a run-list protected by two pool locks.

evidence.quote: __mul_alloc_no_lock walks multiple.freelist and carves n pages; __mul_free_no_lock inserts and merges adjacent runs; kpminit initializes locks and free ranges.
evidence.relevance: Shows real allocation/free logic and initialization of free memory.

evidence.quote: allocpage_n acquires multiple.lock; _allocpage acquires single.lock and falls back to multiple; freepage_n/_freepage call free helpers.
evidence.relevance: Shows allocator public entry points and lock-held paths.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

answer.value: 核心数据结构是 `struct run { next, npage }` 链表节点，以及 `struct pm_allocator { spinlock lock, run *freelist, npage }`；全局有 `multiple` 连续页池和 `single` 单页池。
answer.notes: 答案直接来自物理页分配器结构体和空闲链表操作。

evidence.quote: struct run has next,npage; struct pm_allocator has spinlock lock, run *freelist, npage; global multiple and single allocators.
evidence.relevance: Physical page allocator state is a run-list protected by two pool locks.

answer.value: 锁粒度是两个全局池级自旋锁：`multiple.lock` 保护连续多页 run list，`single.lock` 保护单页 freelist；`allocpage_n/freepage_n` 持有 multiple，`_allocpage` 先持有 single，失败时再持有 multiple。
answer.notes: 没有 per-CPU、分桶或无锁页帧分配；锁范围围绕对应池的 freelist 操作。

evidence.quote: SATP_SV39 and MAKE_SATP define Sv39 page-table activation; PGSIZE=4096; PTE_V/R/W/X/U flags; sfence_vma emits sfence.vma or K210-compatible encoding.
evidence.relevance: Defines page size, PTE bits, satp format, and local TLB flush primitive.

evidence.quote: walk allocates lower-level page tables with allocpage and writes PTE_V; mappages writes PA2PTE(pa)|perm|PTE_V; unmappages optionally freepage and clears *pte.
evidence.relevance: Core page-table walk/map/unmap implementation mutates PTEs.

evidence.quote: kvminit allocates kernel_pagetable and kvmmap maps devices, kernel text/data, trampoline; kvminithart writes satp then sfence.vma.
evidence.relevance: Shows kernel virtual-memory setup and MMU activation.

evidence.quote: proc_pagetable returns kvmcreate(); allocproc allocates kstack/trapframe and p->pagetable; proc_freepagetable removes segments and frees page table.
evidence.relevance: Shows per-process page-table creation with kernel mapping copy.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

answer.value: 关键入口：`walk()` 逐级查找/创建页表页；`mappages()` 写入叶子 PTE；`unmappages()` 清 PTE 并可释放物理页。相关辅助入口还有 `uvmalloc()` 和 `uvmdealloc()`。
answer.notes: 列出的是直接修改页表的核心 API。

evidence.quote: walk allocates lower-level page tables with allocpage and writes PTE_V; mappages writes PA2PTE(pa)|perm|PTE_V; unmappages optionally freepage and clears *pte.
evidence.relevance: Core page-table walk/map/unmap implementation mutates PTEs.

evidence.quote: uvmcopy with cow marks writable PTEs as PTE_COW and clears PTE_W, maps the same PA into the child, then sfence_vma.
evidence.relevance: Fork copy path implements COW marking.

evidence.quote: handle_store_page_fault_cow either makes a sole page writable or allocates/copies a new page, clears PTE_COW, writes *ptep, and flushes.
evidence.relevance: Store fault resolves COW.

evidence.quote: sfence_vma flushes local TLB entries using sfence.vma on QEMU or a K210-compatible encoded instruction plus fence.i.
evidence.relevance: Local TLB flush primitive.

evidence.conclusion: Search found local sfence_vma and IPI primitives for boot/wakeup, but no live remote TLB shootdown or remote sfence handler.

answer.value: 页表修改本身未见每进程地址空间锁或页表锁；`mappages/unmappages/uvmprotect/handle_page_fault` 直接改 PTE，改后通过本地 `sfence_vma()` 刷新。页引用计数另由 `page_ref_lock` 保护，不等同于页表锁。
answer.notes: 并发控制证据显示只有引用计数锁和本地 TLB 刷新，未发现专门的页表临界区。

evidence.quote: kvminit allocates kernel_pagetable and kvmmap maps devices, kernel text/data, trampoline; kvminithart writes satp then sfence.vma.
evidence.relevance: Shows kernel virtual-memory setup and MMU activation.

evidence.quote: proc_pagetable returns kvmcreate(); allocproc allocates kstack/trapframe and p->pagetable; proc_freepagetable removes segments and frees page table.
evidence.relevance: Shows per-process page-table creation with kernel mapping copy.

evidence.quote: scheduler writes satp to tmp->pagetable and sfence_vma before swtch, then writes satp to kernel_pagetable and flushes again.
evidence.relevance: Shows runtime address-space switching with satp.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

answer.value: 共享同一页表（内核映射常驻，高半核等）
answer.notes: 每个进程页表由 `kvmcreate()` 复制 kernel_pagetable，内核映射常驻在进程页表；调度时仍会在进程页表与 kernel_pagetable 间切换 satp。

evidence.quote: EXCP_INST_PAGE, EXCP_LOAD_PAGE, EXCP_STORE_PAGE are defined; is_page_fault selects page-fault/access exceptions by platform.
evidence.relevance: Identifies page-fault exception numbers.

evidence.quote: handle_excp dispatches store/load/instruction page exceptions to handle_page_fault(kind, r_stval()).
evidence.relevance: Trap layer calls the memory fault handler.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault locates the segment, walks PTE, handles PTE_COW on store, rejects already-valid illegal access, checks pre-marked PTE_U permissions.
evidence.relevance: Main page-fault decision logic.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault dispatches LOAD to loadelf, HEAP/STACK to lazy allocation, and MMAP to handle_page_fault_mmap.
evidence.relevance: Fault path connects segment type to allocator/load/mmap handlers.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault_lazy rounds badaddr down, calls uvmalloc for one page, then sfence_vma; loadelf calls loadseg on fault.
evidence.relevance: Demand/lazy allocation happens at page fault.

evidence.quote: handle_anonymous_shared and handle_file_mmap allocate/load pages and mappages them; handle_page_fault_mmap checks R/W/X against s->flag and dispatches anonymous/file cases.
evidence.relevance: mmap mappings are materialized on page fault.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

answer.value: 链路：`handle_excp()` 将 RISC-V load/store/inst page fault 交给 `handle_page_fault(kind, stval)`；该函数 `locateseg()` 并 `walk()` PTE；HEAP/STACK 走 `handle_page_fault_lazy()` 调 `uvmalloc()`；LOAD 走 `loadseg()`；MMAP 走 `handle_page_fault_mmap()`；最终通过 `mappages()` 写 PTE 并 `sfence_vma()`。
answer.notes: 这是从异常入口到分配/映射核心点的主链路。

evidence.quote: uvmcopy with cow marks writable PTEs as PTE_COW and clears PTE_W, maps the same PA into the child, then sfence_vma.
evidence.relevance: Fork copy path implements COW marking.

evidence.quote: handle_store_page_fault_cow either makes a sole page writable or allocates/copies a new page, clears PTE_COW, writes *ptep, and flushes.
evidence.relevance: Store fault resolves COW.

evidence.quote: copysegs chooses cow except shared mmap and calls uvmcopy over each segment; mmap segments are duplicated with mmapdup.
evidence.relevance: Fork/clone segment-copy call site activates COW.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault locates the segment, walks PTE, handles PTE_COW on store, rejects already-valid illegal access, checks pre-marked PTE_U permissions.
evidence.relevance: Main page-fault decision logic.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

answer.value: implemented
answer.notes: COW 在 fork/clone 的 segment copy 中标 PTE_COW，写 fault 中复制或独占页面后恢复可写。

evidence.quote: newseg records addr, sz, type, flag, mmap but the uvmalloc call is commented out, so segment creation reserves metadata without allocating pages.
evidence.relevance: Segment metadata supports lazy allocation and logical memory organization.

evidence.quote: growproc checks heap segment and boundary, deallocates on shrink, then only updates heap->sz and p->pbrk on growth.
evidence.relevance: brk/sbrk grow virtual heap metadata without immediate page allocation.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault_lazy rounds badaddr down, calls uvmalloc for one page, then sfence_vma; loadelf calls loadseg on fault.
evidence.relevance: Demand/lazy allocation happens at page fault.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault dispatches LOAD to loadelf, HEAP/STACK to lazy allocation, and MMAP to handle_page_fault_mmap.
evidence.relevance: Fault path connects segment type to allocator/load/mmap handlers.

evidence.quote: sys_sbrk/sys_brk call growproc; sys_mprotect validates page alignment/prot and calls uvmprotect after partofseg.
evidence.relevance: Memory syscalls reach heap growth and protection paths.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

fact_answers.notes: newseg/growproc 只记录或扩展虚拟区间。
struct seg 记录 addr/sz/type/flag。
HEAP/STACK 缺页时调用 uvmalloc 分配一页。
mprotect 和 fault path 检查权限/边界。
lazy allocation 是 HEAP/STACK 的 fault-time allocation，不是 COW 标记本身。
覆盖通用内存关键词。

answer.value: implemented
answer.notes: brk/heap 与 segment 元数据先保留虚拟范围，物理页延迟到 fault。

evidence.conclusion: Search hits are documentation, sysinfo fields, filesystem/cache terms, and a commented-out __page_file_swap block; no live swap backing store, swap_in/out, pageout, replacement, or cleaner path was found.

evidence.quote: handle_anonymous_shared and handle_file_mmap allocate/load pages and mappages them; handle_page_fault_mmap checks R/W/X against s->flag and dispatches anonymous/file cases.
evidence.relevance: mmap mappings are materialized on page fault.

answer.value: TLB 刷新点是 `sfence_vma()`，QEMU 下执行 `sfence.vma`，K210 下用编码指令加 `fence.i`；调用点包括 `kvminithart()`、`uvmcopy()`、`uvmprotect()`、mmap/fault 路径、scheduler satp 切换和 exec satp 切换。
answer.notes: 回答聚焦本地 TLB flush；未发现 remote shootdown。

evidence.quote: walkaddr rejects va>=MAXUVA, missing/invalid PTEs, and PTEs without PTE_U before returning a physical address.
evidence.relevance: Software user pointer translation checks user-accessible PTEs.

evidence.quote: copyout2/copyin2 require partofseg before safememmove; safememmove temporarily permit_usr_mem then restores protect_usr_mem and reports faults.
evidence.relevance: User copy helpers combine segment-range checks with hardware access protection.

evidence.quote: sys_sbrk/sys_brk call growproc; sys_mprotect validates page alignment/prot and calls uvmprotect after partofseg.
evidence.relevance: Memory syscalls reach heap growth and protection paths.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault locates the segment, walks PTE, handles PTE_COW on store, rejects already-valid illegal access, checks pre-marked PTE_U permissions.
evidence.relevance: Main page-fault decision logic.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

answer.value: 用户指针安全检查由两层组成：`copyin2/copyout2/copyinstr2` 先用 `partofseg/locateseg` 验证用户虚拟范围，再由 `safememmove()` 临时允许访问用户内存并依赖 fault escape；旧式 `walkaddr()` 也显式要求 `va < MAXUVA`、PTE 有效且含 `PTE_U`。
answer.notes: 软件区间检查与硬件 PTE/SUM-PUM 保护共同构成用户指针保护。

answer.value: 逻辑组织：ELF `LOAD` 段按程序头建立并记录 flags；heap 位于最后一个 LOAD 后 `PGROUNDUP(...)+PGSIZE`，通过 brk/sbrk 扩展；stack 固定在 `VUSTACK` 下方 35 页；mmap 区从 `VUMMAP`/`VUSTACK` 附近向高地址扫描放置，也支持 MAP_FIXED 覆盖指定区间；segment 链表记录各区域。
answer.notes: 答案来自 exec 建段、growproc、mmap placement 和 memlayout 常量。

answer.value: 放置策略：brk/heap 从 ELF 最后 LOAD 后一页开始，增长到下一个 segment 前的边界；匿名或非固定 mmap 默认从 `VUMMAP` 开始，在 stack 后向高地址扫描第一个足够 gap；`MAP_FIXED` 使用调用者指定起止地址并分裂/删除重叠 segment。
answer.notes: 虚拟地址选择由 exec/growproc/lookup_segment/lookup_fixed_segment 共同决定。

evidence.conclusion: Search found no working-set model, thrashing prevention, resident-set controller, reclaim daemon, or memory load-control path.

evidence.quote: handle_excp dispatches store/load/instruction page exceptions to handle_page_fault(kind, r_stval()).
evidence.relevance: Trap layer calls the memory fault handler.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault locates the segment, walks PTE, handles PTE_COW on store, rejects already-valid illegal access, checks pre-marked PTE_U permissions.
evidence.relevance: Main page-fault decision logic.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault dispatches LOAD to loadelf, HEAP/STACK to lazy allocation, and MMAP to handle_page_fault_mmap.
evidence.relevance: Fault path connects segment type to allocator/load/mmap handlers.

evidence.quote: handle_page_fault_lazy rounds badaddr down, calls uvmalloc for one page, then sfence_vma; loadelf calls loadseg on fault.
evidence.relevance: Demand/lazy allocation happens at page fault.

evidence.quote: handle_anonymous_shared and handle_file_mmap allocate/load pages and mappages them; handle_page_fault_mmap checks R/W/X against s->flag and dispatches anonymous/file cases.
evidence.relevance: mmap mappings are materialized on page fault.

evidence.quote: walk allocates lower-level page tables with allocpage and writes PTE_V; mappages writes PA2PTE(pa)|perm|PTE_V; unmappages optionally freepage and clears *pte.
evidence.relevance: Core page-table walk/map/unmap implementation mutates PTEs.

evidence.conclusion: General memory-management search covered kernel/include/doc and found live allocator/page-table/protection code; no Rust/crate implementation, buddy/bitmap/CR3 live implementation was found.

answer.value:
```mermaid
graph TD
  A[trap.c handle_excp page exception] --> B[vm.c handle_page_fault kind, stval]
  B --> C[locateseg + walk PTE]
  C --> D{segment type}
  D -->|HEAP/STACK| E[handle_page_fault_lazy]
  E --> F[uvmalloc allocpage]
  D -->|LOAD| G[handle_page_fault_loadelf]
  G --> H[loadseg allocpage + file read]
  D -->|MMAP| I[handle_page_fault_mmap]
  I --> J[anonymous/file mmap allocator]
  F --> K[mappages writes PTE]
  H --> K
  J --> K
  K --> L[sfence_vma]
```
answer.notes: 主链路从缺页异常到物理页分配、PTE 写入和本地 TLB flush。

evidence.quote: struct run has next,npage; struct pm_allocator has spinlock lock, run *freelist, npage; global multiple and single allocators.
evidence.relevance: Physical page allocator state is a run-list protected by two pool locks.

evidence.quote: __mul_alloc_no_lock walks multiple.freelist and carves n pages; __mul_free_no_lock inserts and merges adjacent runs; kpminit initializes locks and free ranges.
evidence.relevance: Shows real allocation/free logic and initialization of free memory.

evidence.quote: SATP_SV39 and MAKE_SATP define Sv39 page-table activation; PGSIZE=4096; PTE_V/R/W/X/U flags; sfence_vma emits sfence.vma or K210-compatible encoding.
evidence.relevance: Defines page size, PTE bits, satp format, and local TLB flush primitive.

evidence.quote: kvminit allocates kernel_pagetable and kvmmap maps devices, kernel text/data, trampoline; kvminithart writes satp then sfence.vma.
evidence.relevance: Shows kernel virtual-memory setup and MMU activation.

evidence.quote: scheduler writes satp to tmp->pagetable and sfence_vma before swtch, then writes satp to kernel_pagetable and flushes again.
evidence.relevance: Shows runtime address-space switching with satp.

evidence.quote: __file_mmapdel, when sync and writable shared mapping has a valid page, calls ip->fop->write for the mapped page before closing.
evidence.relevance: Synchronous mmap writeback on unmap/segment deletion.

evidence.quote: do_msync accepts shared non-anonymous MMAP, iterates mmap_page nodes, and writes map->pa back through ip->fop->write when map->pa exists.
evidence.relevance: Explicit msync writeback path.
